covered_sentences: But once outside the front door, he hesitated.
I called encouragement to him from the kitchen window.
"You had better go home, sir. Miss has a very bad temper indeed."
He turned abruptly and ran back into the sitting room.
When I went into the sitting room to tell them that Hindley was coming home drunk, I saw that a quarrel had only served to make them closer.
It was clear that they were no longer merely friends; they were lovers.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right two-panel split, ratio 1:2 (right panel larger — the lovers' revelation is the narrative climax; left panel shows the exterior hesitation and housekeeper's call). Left panel: camera positioned at the Thrushcross Grange kitchen interior, looking outward through the kitchen window at a low angle; the kitchen window frame forms a natural border — the housekeeper leans toward the window from inside at the lower-left of the frame, her face and upper body partially framed by the window; through the window Edgar is visible outside in the foreground, standing at the front door area looking hesitant. Right panel: camera at the Thrushcross Grange drawing room interior from the doorway position, angled 30 degrees inward at medium range, looking toward Edgar and Catherine in the center of the room; the ornate marble fireplace with portrait is in the background; both figures are close together — their posture and expressions make clear they are no longer just friends. Per-panel budget: left 1 recognizable (housekeeper at window) plus Edgar visible through window as smaller figure; right 2 recognizable (Catherine + Edgar in the drawing room).

Left panel: the housekeeper leans toward the kitchen window from inside, her face and upper body framed within the window opening, one hand on the window frame at her shoulder height; her expression is earnest but pragmatic, calling out to Edgar; her speech bubble from the window contains the words "go home, sir" (compressed direct text extract). Through the window behind her: Edgar is visible outside, standing at the front door step, hat in hand, posture turned halfway back toward the door — his back three-quarter view showing his moment of hesitation and sudden reversal as he turns back; a curved motion arrow behind him indicates his abrupt turn back toward the entrance. Right panel: Edgar and Catherine stand close together in the drawing room, facing each other at close range; their postures are turned inward toward each other — Edgar's expression is open and moved, no longer fleeing; Catherine's expression is softer, no longer enraged, perhaps lightly tearful and relieved; the housekeeper is a small background figure in the right panel, having entered the room with news — her posture slightly to the side, arms low, observing them; no speech bubble for the lovers, their proximity and expressions are the entire message; the housekeeper's thought bubble contains a heart sketch with two arrows pointing inward from both sides — absorbing the realization that they are now lovers.

Left panel: Thrushcross Grange kitchen interior at the window; warm kitchen light with stone floor visible below, the thick wooden kitchen table edge at the left background; the window frame forms a natural rectangular view to the exterior; outside Edgar stands on the stone front doorstep area, the garden or gravel approach visible behind him. Right panel: Thrushcross Grange drawing room, daytime; deep red carpet underfoot, ornate marble fireplace with oval gold-framed portrait above it in the background; heavy curtains at the windows admitting warm daylight; the glass chandelier partially visible overhead; a red upholstered chair visible at the side as proportion anchor.

Left panel: warm kitchen amber light inside, cooler exterior grey light visible through the window where Edgar stands outside — the visual contrast reinforces the insider-outsider dynamic of the housekeeper observing. Right panel: warm drawing room daylight, a slightly softer warmer glow overall as the tension resolves into the revelation of love — the palette lightens slightly relative to the crisis panels before.

Housekeeper: deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round practical face with an earnest expression, dark domestic dress with white apron; leaning at the kitchen window — window frame at approximately her shoulder to head height; her figure visible from approximately the waist upward within the window frame; through the window Edgar is visible at smaller scale. Edgar Linton: light golden long curly hair, large calm light-blue eyes, refined elegant face, light-colored gentleman coat; visible through the window in the left panel as a smaller figure — his hat visible in hand, his back three-quarter view; in the right panel he stands close to Catherine in the drawing room at full scale — his head top at approximately adult male height, the red upholstered chair back visible at his waist height in the right panel. Catherine Earnshaw (aristocratic period): dark brown mid-length neatly arranged hair, slender figure, light cream fine dress with delicate pleats reaching ankle; in the right panel close to Edgar — her head top at approximately two-thirds Edgar's height, the chair back at approximately her shoulder height; her expression softer than in previous panels. Proportion anchor: housekeeper at kitchen window with window frame at shoulder-to-head height; in right panel Edgar's head top at full adult male height with chair back at his waist; Catherine's head top at approximately two-thirds of Edgar's height in the drawing room, chair back at approximately her shoulder height.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

Character appearance, clothing details, and scene furniture layout strictly follow reference images; no self-added or modified details; character proportions consistent throughout. White thin straight dividing line between panels, no rounded corners, no shadow, no gradient, no gap; no outer border. Left panel scene fingerprint: large cooking hearth with iron pots + thick wooden kitchen table + stone floor, position and form unchanged; kitchen window frame as foreground element. Right panel scene fingerprint: ornate marble fireplace + oval gold-framed portrait above fireplace + deep red carpet, position and form unchanged. Housekeeper: deep brown pinned hair must not become loose or light; white apron must not be removed; dark dress must not change to light. Edgar Linton: light golden long curly hair must not change to dark or straight; light coat must not change to dark; no beard or stubble. Catherine (aristocratic period): light cream dress must not change to dark; hair must not become disheveled; not adult tall proportions; softer expression than in previous panels. Proportion anchor: housekeeper at kitchen window frame at shoulder-to-head height; Edgar at full adult male height in right panel with chair back at his waist; Catherine's head at two-thirds of Edgar's height. Correct English spelling; no floating text outside speech bubbles; bubbles must not cover main subjects. All English letters use handwritten style: casual handwritten letterforms, bold strokes, slightly uneven baseline, natural spacing.
